com.mentor.chs.plugin.library

Interface IXLibraryModificationHistoryProvider<O extends IXLibraryObject>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXModifier

    public interface IXLibraryModificationHistoryProvider<O extends IXLibraryObject>
extends IXModifier
    All custom modification history note provider will implement this interface. Object modifier plugin (IXObjectModifierPlugin) implementer will return a set of modifiers which can contain object of type IXLibraryModificationHistoryProvider. It will be used by the application to retrieve an appropriate modification note for the provided modified object. Notes provided by the modifier object will be applied and added to the existing parts modification notes list

    Sample usage of the implementation class: 

                    public class ComponentMaintenanceConnectorModificationHistoryProvider implements IXLibraryModificationHistoryProvider<IXLibraryConnector>{
                        public public String getModificationNote(IXLibraryConnector libObj)
                        {
                                //Special processing for providing appropriate modification note string
                                return modStr;
                        }
                };
    See Also:
    IXModifier, IXObjectModifierPlugin

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getModificationNote(O libraryObject)
      This API returns the note which should be added to the modification history as part of the operation like edit, copy or import.

    • Method Detail

      • getModificationNote

        java.lang.String getModificationNote(O libraryObject)
        This API returns the note which should be added to the modification history as part of the operation like edit, copy or import. If it return null or empty then that note will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        libraryObject - O given part
        Returns:
        String the modification note. It can be null or empty which means its ignored Even if there is a current note as part of component maintenance it will be overwritten with the plugin provided note. If the plugin wants to make use of the current note then it can append to it as needed and provide a new note back.
